rationale: Subject is the large, multi-section all-hip roofed home in the center of the image, identified by its freeform pool to the east. Bounding box estimated at ~365×290 px (77.5×61.6 ft = 4,775 sq ft plan area). Applied ~66% fill factor to account for the complex re-entrant inter-wing cutouts typical of this multi-pyramid hip roof style, yielding ~3,152 sq ft footprint. Five ridge segments identified atop each main hip section (~295 px total). Approximately 14 diagonal hip edges traced from ridge ends/peaks to eave corners (~910 px). Five valley lines identified at interior intersections of roof planes (~270 px). No rake edges present (all-hip construction). Eave perimeter estimated by tracing lower horizontal edges around the complex plan (~800 px). Confidence is moderate-low due to roof complexity, steep viewing angle, and difficulty distinguishing individual section boundaries.
